findings.evidence: Lines 65-87 vs. definitions `bottom := x R.left` / `top := x R.right` (Rectangle.lean:271-276); contrast line 142 which unfolds the same defs by equation lemma.
findings.fix: Replace each `change` with `simp only [GridRectangleBetween.bottom, GridRectangleBetween.top]` (as already done in `transpose_injective`, line 142) or add a comment justifying the defeq.
findings.issue: `transpose`'s `map_left`/`map_right`/`map_of_ne` fields use `change y.transpose (x R.left) = ...` (lines 69, 73, 84) to silently unfold `GridRectangleBetween.bottom`/`top` (= `x R.left`/`x R.right`); no comment documents why a rewrite cannot reach the goal.

findings.evidence: Lines 101-113; the same `bottom`/`top` defeq unfolds are performed by name in `simp only` at line 142.
findings.fix: Drive these with `simp only [GridRectangleBetween.bottom, transpose_left, GridState.transpose_apply, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]` or document the `change` steps.
findings.issue: `transpose_bottom`/`transpose_top` (lines 102, 104, 109, 112) again use undocumented `change` to unfold `bottom`/`top` across the wrapper rather than rewriting with the equation lemma.

summary: Proofs are mostly robust and readable, but the diagonal-reflection definition and its side-projection lemmas rely on several undocumented `change` tactics that unfold the `bottom`/`top` def wrappers via unstated definitional equality.
